mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129750k as of Sept 2015 reflects ongoing post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains positive but SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero (below 0.25%), signaling continued monetary accommodation that suppresses risk-free rates and incentivizes equity allocation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Sept 2015 reflects zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or cumulative excess returns in low-rate periods do not exceed baseline by at least 200 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer demand strength that translates to corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move in the same direction simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363194M and HOUST at 575k units as of Aug 2015
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 52% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST increase simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness or demand destruction that reduces corporate profitability expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% as of Sept 2015 reflects tightening labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes purchasing power and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.729 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Sept 2015
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown or incipient recession that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13149.5B (2017 dollars) as of Q2 2015
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 56% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows above 1.0% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously increases above 200k persons, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion that boosts corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 1.0% MoM AND PAYEMS growth > 200k persons MoM
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.61 and PAYEMS at 129750k as of Sept 2015
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 52% of instances where both conditions are met simultaneously.
